name: Invoke-WebRequest

options.name: -AllowUnencryptedAuthentication
options.description: Allows sending of credentials and secrets over unencrypted connections. By default, supplying Credential or any Authentication option with a Uri that doesn't begin with `https://` results in an error and the request is aborted to prevent unintentionally communicating secrets in plain text over unencrypted connections. To override this behavior at your own risk, supply the AllowUnencryptedAuthentication parameter.

options.name: -Authentication
options.description: Specifies the explicit authentication type to use for the request. The default is None . The Authentication parameter can't be used with the UseDefaultCredentials parameter.

options.name: -Body
options.description: Specifies the body of the request. The body is the content of the request that follows the headers. You can also pipe a body value to `Invoke-WebRequest`.

options.name: -Certificate
options.description: Specifies the client certificate that's used for a secure web request. Enter a variable that contains a certificate or a command or expression that gets the certificate.

options.name: -CertificateThumbprint
options.description: Specifies the digital public key certificate (X509) of a user account that has permission to send the request. Enter the certificate thumbprint of the certificate.

options.name: -ContentType
options.description: Specifies the content type of the web request.

options.name: -Credential
options.description: Specifies a user account that has permission to send the request. The default is the current user.

name: -CustomMethod
-CM
options.description: Specifies a custom method used for the web request. This can be used if the Request Method required by the endpoint isn't an available option on the Method . Method and CustomMethod can't be used together.

options.name: -DisableKeepAlive
options.description: Indicates that the cmdlet sets the KeepAlive value in the HTTP header to False . By default, KeepAlive is True . KeepAlive establishes a persistent connection to the server to facilitate subsequent requests.

options.name: -Form
options.description: Converts a dictionary to a `multipart/form-data` submission. Form may not be used with Body . If ContentType is used, it's ignored.

options.name: -Headers
options.description: Specifies the headers of the web request. Enter a hash table or dictionary.

options.name: -HttpVersion
options.description: Specifies the HTTP version used for the request. The default is `1.1`.

options.name: -InFile
options.description: Gets the content of the web request from a file. Enter a path and filename. If you omit the path, the default is the current location.

options.name: -MaximumRedirection
options.description: Specifies how many times PowerShell redirects a connection to an alternate Uniform Resource Identifier (URI) before the connection fails. The default value is 5. A value of 0 (zero) prevents all redirection.

options.name: -MaximumRetryCount
options.description: Specifies how many times PowerShell retries a connection when a failure code between 400 and 599, inclusive or 304 is received. Also see RetryIntervalSec parameter for specifying number of retries.

options.name: -Method
options.description: Specifies the method used for the web request. The acceptable values for this parameter are:

options.name: -NoProxy
options.description: Indicates that the cmdlet shouldn't use a proxy to reach the destination. When you need to bypass the proxy configured in the environment, use this switch. This feature was added in PowerShell 6.0.0.

options.name: -OutFile
options.description: Specifies the output file for which this cmdlet saves the response body. Enter a path and filename. If you omit the path, the default is the current location. The name is treated as a literal path. Names that contain brackets (`[]`) must be enclosed in single quotes (`'`).

options.name: -PassThru
options.description: Indicates that the cmdlet returns the results, in addition to writing them to a file. This parameter is valid only when the OutFile parameter is also used in the command.

options.name: -PreserveAuthorizationOnRedirect
options.description: Indicates the cmdlet should preserve the `Authorization` header, when present, across redirections.

options.name: -Proxy
options.description: Specifies a proxy server for the request, rather than connecting directly to the internet resource. Enter the URI of a network proxy server.

options.name: -ProxyCredential
options.description: Specifies a user account that has permission to use the proxy server specified by the Proxy parameter. The default is the current user.

options.name: -ProxyUseDefaultCredentials
options.description: Indicates that the cmdlet uses the credentials of the current user to access the proxy server that is specified by the Proxy parameter.

options.name: -Resume
options.description: Performs a best effort attempt to resume downloading a partial file. Resume requires OutFile . Resume only operates on the size of the local file and remote file and performs no other validation that the local file and the remote file are the same.

options.name: -RetryIntervalSec
options.description: Specifies the interval between retries for the connection when a failure code between 400 and 599, inclusive or 304 is received. Also see MaximumRetryCount parameter for specifying number of retries. The value must be between `1` and `[int]::MaxValue`.

name: -SessionVariable
-SV
options.description: Specifies a variable for which this cmdlet creates a web request session and saves it in the value. Enter a variable name without the dollar sign (`$`) symbol.

options.name: -SkipCertificateCheck
options.description: Skips certificate validation checks. This includes all validations such as expiration, revocation, trusted root authority, etc.

options.name: -SkipHeaderValidation
options.description: Indicates the cmdlet should add headers to the request without validation.

options.name: -SkipHttpErrorCheck
options.description: This parameter causes the cmdlet to ignore HTTP error statuses and continue to process responses. The error responses are written to the pipeline just as if they were successful.

options.name: -SslProtocol
options.description: Sets the SSL/TLS protocols that are permissible for the web request. By default all, SSL/TLS protocols supported by the system are allowed. SslProtocol allows for limiting to specific protocols for compliance purposes.

options.name: -TimeoutSec
options.description: Specifies how long the request can be pending before it times out. Enter a value in seconds. The default value, 0, specifies an indefinite time-out.

options.name: -Token
options.description: The OAuth or Bearer token to include in the request. Token is required by certain Authentication options. It can't be used independently. Token takes a `SecureString` containing the token. To supply the token manually use the following:

options.name: -TransferEncoding
options.description: Specifies a value for the transfer-encoding HTTP response header. The acceptable values for this parameter are:

options.name: -Uri
options.description: Specifies the Uniform Resource Identifier (URI) of the internet resource to which the web request is sent. Enter a URI. This parameter supports HTTP or HTTPS only.

options.name: -UseBasicParsing
options.description: This parameter has been deprecated. Beginning with PowerShell 6.0.0, all Web requests use basic parsing only. This parameter is included for backwards compatibility only and any use of it has no effect on the operation of the cmdlet.

options.name: -UseDefaultCredentials
options.description: Indicates that the cmdlet uses the credentials of the current user to send the web request. This can't be used with Authentication or Credential and may not be supported on all platforms.

options.name: -UserAgent
options.description: Specifies a user agent string for the web request.

options.name: -WebSession
options.description: Specifies a web request session. Enter the variable name, including the dollar sign (`$`).

description: Gets content from a web page on the internet.
